I firmly believe that both serendipity and strategic planning play significant roles in success perfectly captured by the quote from Roman philosopher Seneca: "Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity." Drawing from my experiences in banking payments real-time analytics cloud delivery and UX low-code-no-code application builders ERP and enterprise integration I am well-positioned to revolutionize finance operations for enterprises of all sizes.

I have cherished every step of my journey so far and my latest chapter marks an exciting transition from a corporate executive to a startup founder. We've only just begun...

company_description:
ImmunOs Therapeutics AG is developing the next generation of immunotherapies that have a role in the innate immune system for cancer and autoimmune diseases. The company is developing therapies that not only have direct anti-tumor effects but also remodel the tumor microenvironment to enhance the efficacy of existing immunotherapies. 

This portfolio of next generation novel immunomodulatory proteins target diverse and key immunoregulatory receptors. Our lead program iosH2 now advancing to Phase 1 studies is a multi-functional fusion protein that blocks specific LILRB (leukocyte immunoglobulin-like) receptors and thereby activates anti-tumor responses in multiple cancers with monotherapy and in combination with checkpoint inhibitors (CTLA-4 PD-1 PD-L1 CD47) and costimulatory agonists (e.g. 4-1BB).

As Group CEO Nick was the driving force behind the partnership which has seen the company go from strength to strength with over 500 staff in offices across six continents and an ever-growing pipeline of projects. 
 
Rebranded as Lightsource bp the larger stronger company is committed to achieving a joint vision to accelerate the low carbon transition and set the shape of the future of global energy delivery. 
 
Nick's background is in finance and investment which has provided him with the expertise and knowledge required to drive Lightsource bp's success and growth over the last eleven years. about:
Amanda Terry is a founder investor and community builder at the intersection of blockchain AI and venture.

She is Co-Founder & COO of Metagood a full-stack tokenization platform for cross-chain issuance and lifecycle management. Alongside Chairman Bill Tai and CEO Danny Yang she launched OnChainMonkey the first 10K NFT collection on Bitcoin and Osura an Ordinals marketplace and built a global community passionate about on-chain ownership and real world asset tokenization.

Amanda is the Managing Partner of ACTAI Unicorn Fund II LP which has invested in 31 companies. She co-founded ACTAI Ventures in 2021 with Bill Tai after angel co-investing with him since 2018. ACTAI Ventures invests primarily in pre-seed and seed stage companies globally that leverage digitization to make industries more efficient and processes more scalable and replicable and technology as a force for good. 

Prior to co-founding ACTAI Ventures and Metagood she was an operator at six companies with successful exits totaling over $47B in combined acquisition value. She led direct response ad product sales at Twitter and was part of the early digital team at TARGUSinfo (acquired by Neustar). She served as COO of Velocity Black a mobile payments & hospitality platform (acquired by Capital One) was among the first U.S. employees at 5Min Media (acquired by AOL) and part of a 2 person partnership team at Zagat (acquired by Google). She also led sales at major strategic accounts at Acxiom prior to and after their acquisition by IPG. She began her career at Trilogy Software gaining early exposure to high-growth technology ventures. 

Beyond her entrepreneurial and investment endeavors Amanda is committed to social impact and mentorship. She is actively involved in the 501(c)(3) ACTAI Global community and is a sought-after speaker in the tech and blockchain industry speaking at SXSW Consensus West Tech Fest NFT NYC NFT LA NFT Paris Bitcoin Unleashed Neckerverse Web3 Summit London Blockchain Finance Summit Cannes Lions and Camp Decrypt. Amanda holds a BA in Public Policy and East Asian Studies from Princeton University and an MBA in Finance and Entrepreneurial Management from The Wharton School at the University of Pennsylvania.
current_position_description:
Metagood is building infrastructure for the tokenization of real-world assets bridging physical and digital value in a secure transparent and compliant way. Co-founded by Bill Tai Danny Yang and Amanda Terry the team pioneered fully on-chain digital assets with OnChainMonkey the first 10K NFT collection written in a single Ethereum transaction and later the first 10K collection inscribed on Bitcoin. Metagood also launched Osura a curated marketplace for Bitcoin Ordinals and has developed a full-stack platform for bringing real-world assets on-chain.

about:
Chas Pulido is a 3rd generation healthcare executive serial entrepreneur & venture capitalist with a passion for helping startups drive patient impact.

Chas founded Alix Ventures where he serves as General Partner to support high potential early stage Life Science startups engineering biology to drive radical advances in human health. He is particularly excited about startups at the intersection of technology & biology known as “TechBio” building platforms for “Novel Applications” (Therapeutics/Diagnostics) & “Bio-Economy Infrastructure” (Services/Manufacturing). 

Recognizing the TechBio movement at hand Chas created BIOS - Content & community platform to unite Life Science industry innovators. To learn more about BIOS & initiatives check out: BIOS Medium (Top Writer - Venture Capital) BIOS Podcast (Top 5 Life Sciences) BIOS Daily Newsletter (25K+ Subscribers) BIOS Angels (World’s 1st TechBio Angel Investing Syndicate) & join our community - BIOS Commons (1000+ Members). 

Chas started his foray into venture capital with the opportunity to support Healthcare/Life Science (HC/LS) investments at Liquid 2 Ventures (L2V) led by Joe Montana - Hall of Fame Quarterback a leading early stage VC fund focused on backing breakout founders - 750+ portfolio companies including standout HC/LS startups such as: Nurx Truepill Cerebral Athelas Solugen H1 Insights Cricket Lively Culture Herophilus Getlabs 64x Bio Xilis amongst others. While @ L2V Chas spent his time sourcing/diligencing new investments & helping portfolio founders excel in business development hiring & fundraising.

Chas began his entrepreneurial pursuits at The University of Notre Dame where he majored in Management Consulting and served as an Innovation Fellow. His interests took him west to Silicon Valley through Y-Combinator with the founding of Pareto Life Sciences the world’s first stem cell marketplace. In an effort to help patients access breakthrough treatments he joined Clara Health (Acquired by M&B Sciences) - a leading clinical trial innovation startup - as an early employee to head business development & operations.  

Outside of work you can find Chas exploring his creativity while cooking painting glassblowing or roaming about hiking exploring museums & traveling (70+ Countries/7 Continents).
company_description:
Alix Ventures: Driving Patient Impact - Supporting early stage Life Science startups engineering biology to drive radical advances in human health

Born from our collective experience as founders we set out to build the fund we wanted to raise from...

Thesis: Life Sciences is experiencing rapid innovation with the rise of new generation of companies known as "TechBio" (Merging technology & biology) a change we believe is leading to some of the most impactful solutions in decades. Our passion is to help build transformative companies ahead of this revolution to significantly impact the quality & effectiveness of patient care. We aim to partner with passionate accomplished founders who possess unique insights to solve some of the world's most daring problems facing us in Life Sciences.

Team: Our entire team is built of Life Science Founders with experience in raising capital scaling teams running labs partnering with Pharma & the many fires that consist of operating an early stage company. Founders we'd love to partner with you along the journey & help translate daunting ideas into world shaping innovations - here's to a better brighter future for patients! 

SAB: Composed of Bioengineering PhDs turned Founders our Scientific Advisory Board provides deep technical expertise & fluency across a wide variety of Life Science sub-domains from platforms for "Novel Applications" (Therapeutics/Diagnostics) to "Bio-Economy Infrastructure" (Tools/Services/Manufacturing). Let's talk science!

Support: Helping our founders is our utmost priority. To tackle the many challenges ahead we've curated a full platform of resources to help with customer introductions fundraising & team building amongst others. Seed capital for the unusual

Our Investment Thesis
Fiddlehead Ventures follows two distinct investment theses - Forward Thinging and Deadpool. The Forward Thinging approach focuses on investing in things before they become "a thing". Foraging the international technology landscape for the most intriguing and promising niche opportunities. The Deadpool approach leverages Darryl Kirsh’s experience as a 30+ year turnaround and restructuring professional to identify undervalued failing and struggling tech companies where significant resources have already been invested and real assets and value exist.

"Forward Thinging"
 Fiddlehead Ventures seeks to identify what's new exciting and with plenty of potential for growth before it's in the mainstream. That means beating the bushes and imagining the business landscape 10 years out. In particular we keep our eyes open for niches often overlooked simply because they don't show up on the radar and are often considered boring unappealing and not part of the latest wave. Fortunately these often make the most profitable investments.

His innovations have contributed to nearly all "next generation" DNA sequencing methods and companies (CGI-BGI Life Illumina Nanopore). This plus his lab's work on chip-DNA-synthesis gene editing and stem cell engineering resulted in founding additional application-based companies spanning fields of medical diagnostics ( Knome/PierianDx Alacris Nebula Genomics Veritas Genetics ) & synthetic biology / therapeutics (AbVitro/Juno Gen9/Ginkgo Editas Egenesis enEvolv WarpDrive ). He has also pioneered new privacy biosafety ELSI environmental & biosecurity policies. He is director of an IARPA BRAIN Project and NIH Center for Excellence in Genomic Science. His honors include election to NAS & NAE & Franklin Bower Laureate for Achievement in Science. He has coauthored 460 papers 105 patent publications & one book (Regenesis).

about:
My background as a research scientist started with my Ph.D. work under Prof. Toshi Ishikawa which focused on multidrug resistance against anti-tumor agents. The goal of my research was ultimately to design a new drug molecule that circumvents multidrug resistance. Eventually I continued to conduct studies with Prof. Osamu Nureki and I collaborated with a pharma company Nihon Chemiphar Co. Ltd. to screen new compounds which resulted in a successfully granted patent. I have been interested in translating academic research into industry R&D since I was a Ph.D. student. After completing my Ph.D. work I started my career at Astellas as a scientist at Pharmaceutical Research and Technology laboratories where I developed formulations of small molecules and engineered biologics for investigational and commercial use. I was also involved in process development and validation of aseptic biological products for GMP manufacturing. As Astellas started shifting their research interest into new modalities in 2013 I joined an interesting project looking at pharmaceutical applications of Japanese fundamental research done by a small biotech company on recombinant fibrinogen made using transgenic silkworms. This experience was extremely helpful in my subsequent business development and investment activities in cell and gene therapy.
After a 6-year tenure as a scientist I pivoted my scientific career into a global business professional. Following an internal competition I was selected to join an entrepreneurial business development division which was newly established at Astellas called as Astellas Innovation Management ("AIM"). Following my tenure at AIM I moved to the USA to join Astellas Venture Management ("AVM") the corporate venture capital arm of Astellas based in Silicon Valley. thereover my time at AVM I was responsible for the end-to-end deal process on all opportunities globally in RNA cell and gene therapy performing due diligence transacting deals and providing investee companies with input from the pharma perspective as a board observer. I screened 1000s of opportunities closed equity financings and debt financings in investee companies made fund investments as a limited partner and led strategic partnerships with US and EU-based venture capital funds accelerators and institutions. I am motivated by my curiosity of emerging science and technology and through my interactions with founding researchers and entrepreneurs to build new innovative companies that will grow in value and transform healthcare.

about:
I am an investor who used to be a startup founder. As a Managing Director with Aventurine I am looking to invest early in companies with strong IP based on breakthrough technology. 


My background:

I co-founded my first company BraveTree in 2001. At BraveTree we developed multi-player games for the web and for XBox Live Arcade. During this time I was also very active in the independent game development community acting as an evangelist for independent games blogging and speaking at independent game conferences.

We sold BraveTree to it's publisher GarageGames in 2005. I was brought on to join the executive team at GarageGames to build a team focused on developing our own internal games and IP and work with the Engine Products team to extend the lines of our educational products. I managed the game division of the company including management of our 3rd party games portfolio.

I helped to grow that company until it was acquired by IAC in 2007. I stayed on after the merger to help manage the transition and explosive growth that followed finally leaving in 2009 to pursue other interests.

I co-founded a biotech incubator and was then asked to stand up an accelerator program as part of a state funded initiative called RAIN (the Regional Accelerator and Innovation Network)

As Managing Director of the RAIN Eugene Accelerator and Executive Director of the associated non-profit RAIN Eugene I created programs to help foster creation of high growth companies in Oregon and accelerate the state's transition from a resource-based to a knowledge-based economy.

I then transitioned my activities to the creation of the Coast to Crest Fund. At Coast to Crest we provided capital to high growth startups at the earliest stages. 

After Coast to Crest went into harvest mode (we are no longer actively investing) - I joined the Aventurine Capital Group to raise a fund to invest in early stage technology heavy startups that can be monetized through IP licensing. about:
On a mission to humanize technology before it dehumanizes us.

I invest in pre-seed and seed-stage startups building human-centric AI across Health & Wellness the Future of Work and Sustainable Living. I look for AI that unlocks human potential enables entirely new use cases in massive markets and is defensible with strong moats.

As an exited AI founder I partner closely with founders—bringing deep AI expertise and a hands-on platform to help companies move faster build smarter and scale with intention.

I am also the Host of Pioneers of AI a weekly show where I sit down with leading founders thinkers and business leaders to explore how AI is reshaping business creativity science and society—and what it means to stay human along the way.

My journey into AI has been anything but linear (I share it in my book Girl Decoded Penguin Random House). I grew up in Egypt and the Middle East earned my PhD in machine learning and computer vision at Cambridge and completed my postdoc at MIT. In 2009 I co-founded Affectiva out of the MIT Media Lab pioneering the category of Emotion AI. Affectiva was acquired by Smart Eye in 2021.

I am deeply committed to ethical inclusive and responsible AI and to expanding who gets to build—and benefit from—these technologies.

I serve as a Trustee of the Museum of Science (Boston) and Milton Academy am a World Economic Forum Young Global Leader and Eisenhower Fellow and am active in YPO where I helped launch and chair YPO Boston and co-chaired YPO's first AI Summit. I also co-chair the Fortune Brainstorm AI conferences am a TED speaker and co-hosted a PBS NOVA series on AI.

Above all I believe in the power of human connection.
I am a Chief Possibility Officer. Holler if you want to co-create some possibilities together.

about:
Truv builds customer-permissioned products that help lenders verify income at prices 80% lower than the big incumbents.

That's a fancy way of saying your customers should control their income data and you shouldn’t overpay for income verification.

Truv’s product optimizes conversion by customizing the verification type for each customer.

How it works:

1/ We built 3 ways to verify income: (a) Payroll login (b) Bank Login (c) Document Upload.

2/ We use Smart Routing to decide if a customer goes through the Payroll login or the Bank login.

This model was trained on over >10M of transactions.

Smart Routing optimizes conversion based on what we know about the customer and the 300 000+ employers we’ve seen.

3/ We use our Document Upload process for cases where it doesn't make sense to send the customer to a Payroll or Bank verification. Or when a customer doesn't complete the verification.

4/ Our coverage boasts 92% of working Americans and can verify 60-80% of applicants instantly.

5/ The remaining cases are hard to automate. These customers get verified through phone calls instant databases or simply drop out of the purchase funnel due to low intent or inability to verify income.

Want to see Truv’s product in action?

DM or email at kirill@truv.com for a demo.
company_description:
Truv is the industry-leading consumer-permissioned data platform with unmatched coverage compliance and data quality. With Truv your customers easily verify income and employment assets insurance switch direct deposit and more at 60-80% cost savings compared to traditional verification methods. 

We're proud to be a Freddie Mac Loan Product Advisor® asset and income modeler (AIM) provider and Fannie Mae Desktop Underwriter® (DU®) validation service provider—the only consumer-permissioned VOIE platform with both accreditations solidifying our commitment to delivering top-notch verification services tailored for mortgage lenders banks and credit unions.

about:
I have been in the venture capital industry for nearly thirty years having joined a venture firm in the early '90s straight out of undergrad when the venture and entrepreneurial ecosystems were very different (perhaps explaining why someone with no real experience could get a job in VC). I benefited from great mentors and subsequently co-founded two venture firms including Osage University Partners (OUP) in 2009. 

OUP focuses on investing in companies that are turning ground-breaking research into game-changing products. Discoveries do not instantaneously transform into great products that enterprises go out of their way to purchase. OUP has developed extensive knowledge and a few battle scars regarding how to translate technology into products and how to find the right market for those products. We invest at lots of stages from hands-on seed stage investing to later stage growth rounds. OUP is also mission driven to help the deeptech entrepreneurial ecosystem.

Through my career I have invested in over seventy companies across multiple sectors including software hardware cleantech life science and way back when consumer. 

Feel free to contact me at msinger@oup.vc.

about:
Matt Ocko with three decades of experience as a technology entrepreneur and VC in the US and globally is co-Managing Partner and co-founder of DCVC (Data Collective). Matt's current investments on behalf of DCVC span from computational drug discovery and synthetic biology to geospatial and space access platforms robotics applied AI anti-terror systems and large-scale enterprise platforms including quantum computers. He is a proud member of BENS (Business Executives for National Security) led by Gen. (ret) Joseph Votel and informally advises a number of US government entities on technology and national security matters.

In addition to large IPO outcomes many of Matt's prior investments were acquired to become core capabilities of companies like Illumina Cisco Google IBM Amazon/AWS VMware Salesforce and Akamai. His venture investments prior to DCVC include Zoom (ZM) Fortinet (FTNT) D-Wave Systems Uber (UBER) AngelList Xensource (CTRX) and Facebook (FB). Matt founded and was VP of R&D of Da Vinci Systems a pioneering e-mail software vendor with over 1 million users world-wide prior to its acquisition.

Matt holds a degree in Physics from Yale University and he is an inventor on over 50 granted or in-process patents in areas as diverse as computer systems virtualization fraud detection and AR. He still writes code and builds robots for fun with his kids but admits to liking Erlang better than Clojure and Fischertechnik better than Lego Mindstorms.

about:
Mose Cassaro is the Founder and GP of RDF Ventures an early stage venture fund backing outlier founders building bold technologies across Vermont. As New England's youngest Solo GP and accelerator founder he is helping shape the future of innovation in rural America through strategic capital deployment and accelerator-backed venture development. Mose also serves as a Program Partner and Manager at global accelerator and venture fund gener8tor supporting startups and entrepreneurs across dozens of markets both national and international. 

Additionally Mose serves as Founder of FoundersOnly the evolution of his work building Startup Rutland in partnership with the Rutland Chamber (CEDRR) to establish Vermont's first globally recognized accelerator program in collaboration with gener8tor. This work now expands statewide through FoundersOnly bridging public private and academic partners to strengthen the region's early-stage entrepreneurial ecosystem and connect rural founders to the national venture landscape.

In addition to his work with gener8tor and FoundersOnly Mose serves on the boards of several startups including Brightwater Tools (SOSV-backed water tech) and has been an operator at venture-backed companies including Viggo (Acq: BOLT) and more. With over a decade of experience across North America EMEA and APAC he's supported founders from ideation through fundraising GTM and scale combining operational depth with strategic execution.

His path into technology began through curiosity and problem solving often led by boredom. By eight he was soldering motherboards and experimenting with hardware. By twelve he had launched iHelp a profitable tech-help business and blog born from his experiments in hacking PSPs and jailbreaking iPhones. He later dropped out of middle school to build his first startup before earning a BBA in Entrepreneurship from Hofstra University where he built a universal interface for digital wallets.

Beyond his work in venture Mose has a deep interest in history theology philosophy and anthropology exploring how belief systems past and present human behavior and cultural evolution shape relationships and civilizations.

At the core of his work is a conviction that technology + capital when guided by wisdom and humility can serve as instruments of human progress. He believes that while true equalization may be unattainable the very pursuit of striving toward an ever-moving ideal creates greater good. It is through that pursuit that we inch closer to a better more just world.

about:
Visionary leader with extensive experience driving growth and innovation in e-commerce fintech and marketplace environments. I excel in building and scaling businesses fostering strategic partnerships and delivering impactful financial and operational solutions for entrepreneurs and small businesses.

As a dynamic operator with over six years of experience in startup ecosystems I specialize in turning vision into execution. From spearheading growth strategies to driving operational excellence I have a proven track record of scaling ventures optimizing performance and building high-performing teams. My passion lies in empowering entrepreneurs and creating sustainable growth through data-driven insights and innovative marketplace strategies.

 Scaled e-commerce and marketplace ventures through innovative business development and growth strategies.

Fostered partnerships with leading platforms to help entrepreneurs access the resources they need to succeed.

Delivered measurable results in revenue growth market expansion and team leadership.

Deep expertise in capital formation fundraising and strategic vision for emerging businesses.

Out Of The Office: Outside of my professional life my two great passions are surfing and high-performance driving. The self-inflicted chaos of speeding down a straightway towards a corner at 130 MPH or dropping into a 10-foot wave on a Sunday make fires in the office on Monday laughably manageable. company_description:
ECOR develops high-value recycling technologies and manufactures advanced green building materials. 

ECOR is a sustainable alternative to a range of traditional building materials including MDF HDF and Plywood. It is used by architects designers and product developers for a range of applications including furniture flooring and secondary packaging. 
 
ECOR offers true competitive advantages over traditional materials as it is designed to be higher performance durable clean (VOC-free) green (made from up to 100% recycled materials + recyclable) and ultimately cost less or the same. Using ECOR instead of virgin materials also saves trees reduces pollution and landfill waste.
 
Integrated ECOR solutions uniquely provide enterprise manufacturing and government partners a turn-key solution to recoup substantial revenues from their waste streams by creating a closed-loop system. These solutions are ideal for regional governments and manufacturers that want to engage in the circular economy reduce their environmental impact and convert their waste into wealth.
 
Learn more at ecorglobal.com and get in touch with us at info@ecorglobal.com if you'd like to learn more about the company or are interested in using ECOR.

I am Managing General Partner & founder at SOSV a venture capital firm with more than $1.5B in assets under management. SOSV pioneers & runs world-leading startup development programs. We help fund at the Pre-seed/seed stage 70 new startups per year out of the over 5 000 that apply to our deep-tech HAX and IndieBio startup development programs/studios... We follow-on invest into SOSV-backed companies that achieve investment and marketplace traction at seed Series A and occasionally Series B. 

I have worked as an engineer musician computer programmer software designer marketing guy sales guy humanitarian worker war photographer cinematographer on music videos short films & documentaries stock picker freelance photojournalist small business operator venture capitalist and writer. Co-inventor and popularizer (with my other MapInfo friends) of street mapping on personal computers. I have over 30 granted patents where I am listed as the primary inventor. I am credited with the co-invention (with George Favaloro of Compaq) of the term "cloud computing"​. Also coined the term "Protein farming". Rough tourist-level Spanish pidgeon French & Arabic & Chinese.

I was a TV personality in Ireland for a few years as an investor on the TV reality show "Dragon's Den" and founded and ran the humanitarian organization JumpStart International for a couple of years in a couple of war zones. I backed Coderdojo and Khan Academy in their crucial early years. I'm still on the board of Khan Academy. I'm an organizing member of the Autism Impact Fund and on the social causes side I look to address breakthroughs in autism treatment options particularly on the physiology of autism.

Specialties: helping SOSV-backed founders go further & faster getting other people interested in things I care about improving efficiency in processes creating technology to solve real problems for real human beings.

Office Hours

company_description:
SOSV is the most active deep tech venture capital firm globally investing at pre-seed (or at startup inception) supporting visionary founders intent on making a profound impact on human and planetary health.

SOSV has three deeply resourced locations for founders in San Francisco (SOSV SF) New York (SOSV NY) and Newark (SOSV HAX). Each year SOSV makes roughly 60 pre-seed investments of up to $550K each as well as another 150 follow-on investments in deep tech companies. The pre-seed founders spend 4–6 months working side-by-side with SOSV General Partners and the broader team of experts in science and engineering design and product development supply chain and product-market fit. Additionally founders gain access to each location's office space and equipped lab space.

With CodeControl and AgentControl teams can update agent behavior in milliseconds automatically roll back when something drifts and govern every agent and feature across the organization from one place. No redeploys required.

LaunchDarkly processes more than 50 trillion flag evaluations per day and serves some of the most demanding engineering teams in the world including over a quarter of the Fortune 500. about:
I am a creator a builder of bridges between ideas and reality. My passion is transforming embryonic ideas into impactful solutions that elevate the human condition.

With a background rooted in biophysical chemistry and computational biophysics I have fostered innovation and spearheaded developments in pharma biotech synthetic biology and beyond.

In 2009 I founded Karyopharm materializing my vision to target nuclear export for cancer treatment. Leading a dedicated team I orchestrated the evolution of the company from conception to a recognized public entity. Our relentless pursuit of excellence culminated in the approval of XPOVIO (NEXPOVIO in EU) a groundbreaking treatment for patients battling myeloma and lymphoma.

With over two decades of experience I’ve steered the development of over 9 novel drug candidates marking significant milestones in drug discovery and development.

E44 my latest venture is a testament to my unwavering commitment to innovation. I am channeling my energy into unearthing and nurturing technologies in Sustainable Energy and Green Tech. By collaborating with academia and early-stage innovators we’re crafting technology-driven companies dedicated to a cleaner more sustainable future.

My networks include artificial intelligence engineering and agriculture. I am propelled by the challenge of intertwining technology and sustainability and at E44 we’re not just witnessing the fusion - we’re sculpting it.

about:
Thrill-Seeker Disrupter Angel. 
Founder MacDonald Ventures

Steve MacDonald is a serial entrepreneur a visionary who nurtures mentors and invests in tech start-ups committed to creating a better world. With more than $400 million in exits in companies he founded his rise to multi-million dollar success started with the founding of TechHealth in 1999 and myMatrixx his pharmacy benefits firm in 2001. Following his 2017 $250mm exit of myMatrixx at the time one of the largest privately owned companies in Tampa Bay Steve launched his next venture MacDonald Ventures where he pursues his passion for startups in the high stakes world of Angel investing.

Named Business Observer’s Entrepreneur of the Year in 2013 Steve has always been driven towards success but his path to it has not been without obstacles. He refuses to back down in the face of unprecedented challenges and credits them with furthering his drive to succeed improving his risk tolerance and instincts and underscoring the importance of paving his own path in business and life.

Steve is heavily invested in the startup ecosystem of Tampa Bay. In 2019 he was named Catalyst’s TiETAN Super Entrepreneur an award that recognized his decades of work as one of Florida’s tech pioneers. Steve serves as an Entrepreneur in Residence at the Tampa Bay Wave a tech startup incubator and as a board member of Florida Funders a hybrid venture capital fund and network of angels that funds and builds early stage technology companies in Florida and beyond. He is frequently asked to speak on investing entrepreneurship and successful startups.

An avid athlete and adventurer Steve provides founders and entrepreneurs with decades of experience limitless drive financial investment mentorship and community. His passion for helping startups thrive as they develop revolutionary technologies is matched only by his resilience and drive to succeed.

#Fundingphenomenons

Bodidata® - Size-Matching Made Easy™

Returns are a multi-billion-dollar problem for Ready-To-Wear ("RTW") apparel companies. Customers report that the #1 reason for returns is wrong size or fit.

Returns have high reverse logistics costs that often lead to a destruction decision as the lowest cost option. This is a growing ESG problem for companies and B-Corps.

Bodidata was created to solve the complex problem of matching different body shapes and sizes to RTW clothing and because we understand the "Complexity of Fit" and the issues associated with matching infinite body shapes into RTW clothing we know that accurate measurement is only one of the requirements for successful size-matching.

A successful size-matching provider needs to measure accurately match skillfully and manage customer expectations. Bodidata has addressed all of these issues with an integrated suite of solutions. We Measure.Match.Manage.™ all three essential requirements to reduce returns.

Bodidata offers every available measurement technology to safely serve different business needs and customer preference. Competitors only offer one. The patented Kora™ scanner is the only handheld solution that measures a fully clothed individual in streetwear.

Bodidata uses a Mobile APP that interactively engages customers increasing stickiness and loyalty to our clients while providing valuable customer insights.

a)The Bodidata Mobile APP includes:
a. Kora™ Registration - the only contact free 3D body measurement solution in streetwear
b. BodiMap® - 3 additional contact free body measurement options
c. Bodi-ID® - Profile Identifier 
d. BodiMatch® - size-matching & recommendations 
e. BodiGuide™ - personalized curation & customer feedback loop and 
f. Event Registration - users register for events.

b)BodiSafari™ a design optimization solution for new & existing products.

Learn more at www.bodidata.com

World-Class Consulting in Transportation and Autonomous Technology

Our consulting services provide unique global expertise in developing and scaling leading platforms within the Transportation and Self-Driving Car sectors. We offer strategic and product leadership grounded in the institutional knowledge that has driven market dominance for pioneering industry companies.

Proven Track Record: From Autonomous Fleets to Hyper-Scaling

Our experience encompasses the critical stages of commercialization and scaling in the most complex and highly regulated technology sectors:

 * Architects of the First Public Autonomous Service: Deep expertise in building the infrastructure for driverless fleets including the launch of the world's first and only public autonomous ride-hailing service. This is the proven methodology for moving from R&D to a paid public multi-city offering.

 * Patented Safety Standards: Experience in developing and filing four patents on autonomous vehicle safety including Psychomotor Vigilance Testing and Fatigue Monitoring Systems. This ensures the creation of a product that is not only scalable but also safe legally defensible and accelerates commercial readiness.

 * Unit Economics Optimization: Successful leadership of initiatives that substantially improved vehicle operator safety compliance resulting in better per-mile economics for the entire fleet.

Mastery of Mobility Ecosystems and Hyper-Growth

Our consulting spans the entire transportation ecosystem and addresses the challenges of exponential growth:
